title: A method to produce borane complexes, reagents for the same, and use of the reagents
abstract: The present invention relates to a diborane-free method for producing borane complexes, such as borane dimethyl sulfide. The method includes reacting boron trifluoride and a Lewis base with a certain tertiary amines, such as 1,2,2,6,6- pentamethylpiperidineunder hydrogen atmosphere. The invention relates also to recovery of side products produced, and reagents for use in preparation of borane complexes.
claims: Claims What is claimed is 1 . A method for producing borane complex, the method comprising - reacting boron trifluoride complex of formula BF3 L, wherein L is a Lewis base, with a tertiary amine and hydrogen, - reacting boron trifluoride and a Lewis base L with a tertiary amine and hydrogen, or - reacting boron trifluoride with a tertiary amine to form a compound of formula BF3- f-Amine, wherein f-Amine is the tertiary amine, and reacting the compound of formula BF3- f-Amine formed with hydrogen, in proviso that intensity of 19 F NMR signal of boron trifluoride etherate does not decrease more than 90% upon admixing with equimolar amount of the tertiary amine. 2. The method according to claim 1 , wherein the intensity of 19 F NMR signal of boron trifluoride etherate does not decrease substantially upon admixing with equimolar amount of the tertiary amine. 3. The method according to claim 2, wherein chemical shift of the 19 F NMR signal of boron trifluoride etherate changes <1 ppm upon admixing with equimolar amount of the tertiary amine. 4. The method according to any of claims 1 -3, wherein the Lewis base L is selected from a group consisting of dialkyi sulfide, aryl alkyl sulfide, tetrahydrofuran, diethyl ether, dimethyl ether, methyl terf-butyl ether and mixtures thereof, preferably from dimethyl sulfide and diethyl ether and mixtures thereof. 5. The method according to any of claims 1 -4, wherein the tertiary amine is 1 ,2,2,6,6- pentamethylpiperidine. 6. The method according to any of claims 1 -5, wherein the tertiary amine is 1 ,2,2,6,6- pentamethylpiperidine, and the reacting comprises reacting the tertiary amine with boron trifluoride prior to reacting with the Lewis base L and hydrogen. 7. The method according to any of claims 1 -6, wherein the reacting is at 30-150 Â°C, preferably at 30 - 100 Â°C, most preferably at 75 Â°C. 8. The method according to any of claims 1 -7, wherein hydrogen pressure is 5-150 bar, preferably 20 bar. 9. The method according to any of claims 1 -8 wherein the borane complex is borane dimethyl sulfide complex. 10. The method according to any of claims 1 -9, wherein the reacting produces tetrafluoroborate salt of the tertiary amine, and wherein the method further comprises isolating the tetrafluoroborate salt of the tertiary amine, preferably by filtration. 1 1 . The method according to claim 10, the method further comprising treating the isolated tetrafluoroborate salt of the tertiary amine with an inorganic base to produce the tertiary amine and inorganic tetrafluoroborate. 12. The method according to claim 1 1 , the method further comprising treating the inorganic tetrafluoroborate with a strong acid such as sulfuric acid, and inorganic boronate to produce boron trifluoride. 13. An admixture comprising a molecule of formula BF3 L and 1 ,2,2,6,6- pentamethylpiperidine, wherein L is a Lewis base. 14. The admixture according to claim 13, wherein the Lewis base L is selected from a group consisting of dimethyl sulfide, tetrahydrofuran, diethyl ether, dimethyl ether, methyl terf-butyl ether, preferably dimethyl sulfide. 15. A molecule of formula Formula (I) f-Amine BF3 (I) wherein f-Amine is selected from a group consisting of and wherein R 1 , R 2 , and R 3 are same or different alkyl groups. 16. The molecule according to claim 15, wherein the f-Amine is 1 ,2,2,6,6- pentamethylpiperidine. 17. Use of the admixture according to claim 13 or 14, or the molecules according to claim 15 or 16 for preparation of borane complexes.
